ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Ní Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «M gbìl̄, ama an kaa sá Isimaayeel bɔti ní m bíi sòoǹ, an sá a-nimpuu Saara di làá màal̀-si kibininjabiki. Á dāá pɔ̄-kì diyindi yii Isaaki. Úǹ nì u-naantiibi bin làá páanní boonee di làá fōō dipoondi din m pōoǹ dí nīn bí kí nīn cá jaanjaanee.
translated: Unimboti said: “I am grieving, for I have lost my son Ishmael, and my wife Sarah has also died. Now I pray for Isaac. May the Almighty grant him a long life and keep him safe from all harm.”

text: Abram nín nìn jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ Keedoolawoomeer nì bibɔtiibi bin nìn dɔ́miń ki kútì u-puee ki fātìi kùn̄ee ní Soodoom bɔtiu túŋkìń-u Saafee tantakalin, an dàkà yii ubɔti tantakalin.
translated: Abram and his family went to Keedoolawoomeer; his children also went, they ate food in Soodoom with Saafee, and then they returned home.

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Niinee Abraam fōō idɔ in u làá tin dūú kpāā kí ŋá saraawɔkaayee u-tɔntɔnliibi cee, ki dūu túǹ u-jipɔɔn Isaaki, ní ki ŋúb́ u-ba ŋŋmi nì kijiki, ní bi-liitil nóó ki nín cá.
translated: When Abraham was a hundred years old, the LORD blessed him and gave him a son, Isaac, and he was very glad.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Ní Unimbɔti bí yii: «Tiwan tin fòotēe kɔkɔ kubuluu kubuluu ń nyā kí nīn bí kitiŋ pu, an dàkà yii tiwaŋkuti nì tin fù ti-biŋŋi puee nì ipeel ń nyā!» Ní an ŋá míǹ.
translated: According to Unimboti: “We must keep moving forward step by step, because the road is long, and we must continue to work together for a better future!”

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Baa làá nīn gíti yíì-si Abram. A-yindi di làá nīn kíǹ ki sá Abraam, kun puee m dū-si ki kpántì atimbun kupaau baa.
translated: Now the LORD said to Abram, “Go out from your country, your people, and your father’s house to the land that I will show you.”

ref: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
text: Isaaki nìn là Eesayuu bɔti kun puee un nìn néeń tipeenan tin Eesayuu nìn cāabìńee. Ní Reebeeka úǹ là Jaakɔb bɔti.
translated: Isaac and Esau were twins, the older was Esau and the younger was Isaac. And Rebecca gave birth to Jacob.

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Ŋyunti ŋun bi nyántì-u ki cáǹ kí wà ŋŋmee ní u tú bí tùkù u-cooja yii: «Tiwan tin m ŋúb́ nee, uninja un tīī-mi dipoolee di yì tiwan gbanti. M gbāam̀-si, kpèè kijalii kin bi cáa nyìitī tiwanee nì ki-ŋmiiŋu nì ŋkpandɔ nee, a nyí un yì tiwan gbanteeee?»

translated: He said: “When Abraham and Sarah were old, what did they say? They said, we have no children, we are old!” Then Hagar gave birth to Ishmael.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.

translated: When Abraham was old and his son Isaac was born, he went to Abimelech’s well to drink water.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Jaakɔb nìn néeń Raseel, ní ki bí u-wee yii: «M làá tɔ̄ ditundi kí tīī-si abin aluli á dū a-bisal uwaal Raseel kí tīī-mi.»
translated: Jacob and his son Raseel, when he said: “I will not go until I have taken care of the people of Raseel; I will stay.”

ref: He put the personal servant back in his old job
text: Ní Faaroo gíiǹ bin bàtī ndaam ki tìì-uee kpiliu tundi ki ŋúbiǹ-u, ní úǹ bātì ndaam ki tīī-u.
translated: The father who works hard and teaches his children, they also help him.
